title: Paul Stamets - On this day in 1943, Albert Hofmann took a... | Facebook
text:
## Paul Stamets's Post

* * *

### [**Paul Stamets**](https://www.facebook.com/PaulStamets?__cft__\[0\]=AZbKeLGu7UtY2xo6_Gj6p9qOMbmWxt__M3AzbAgA0jiO8xfm1aRfMgTtFAsr23BltUnrIArMgoIw_du2z-ayQ1trdJTvBJuLeEM1cvu8DihJ3-Anp9EsfUZthsefICIZfL1VU_F0yKNvo7XhfuO9zYRHnqOgXmB1JD594wLWwKqObw&__tn__=-UC%2CP-R)

[April 19, 2025](https://www.facebook.com/PaulStamets/posts/pfbid0BYMAeoBAFEG74RkwWT52KW5zUfzQMzpnVjH84jL1i5oA68fJppXuCpp5aRugrghQl?__cft__\[0\]=AZbKeLGu7UtY2xo6_Gj6p9qOMbmWxt__M3AzbAgA0jiO8xfm1aRfMgTtFAsr23BltUnrIArMgoIw_du2z-ayQ1trdJTvBJuLeEM1cvu8DihJ3-Anp9EsfUZthsefICIZfL1VU_F0yKNvo7XhfuO9zYRHnqOgXmB1JD594wLWwKqObw&__tn__=%2CO%2CP-R) Â·

On this day in 1943, Albert Hofmann took a now-famous bicycle ride that changed the course of historyâushering in a new era of exploration into consciousness, creativity, and healing.

I had the honor of meeting Albert years ago, and his curiosity, brilliance, and reverence for nature left a lasting impression on me.

Today, on [\](https://www.facebook.com/hashtag/bikeday?__eep__=6&__cft__\[0\]=AZbKeLGu7UtY2xo6_Gj6p9qOMbmWxt__M3AzbAgA0jiO8xfm1aRfMgTtFAsr23BltUnrIArMgoIw_du2z-ayQ1trdJTvBJuLeEM1cvu8DihJ3-Anp9EsfUZthsefICIZfL1VU_F0yKNvo7XhfuO9zYRHnqOgXmB1JD594wLWwKqObw&__tn__=*NK-R) , I reflect on the profound potential of psychedelics to reconnect us with ourselves, with each other, and with the Earth.

Ride on. Expand your mind. Stay curious. And stay safe!

[](https://www.facebook.com/photo/?fbid=1199510371543895&set=pcb.1199510478210551&__cft__\[0\]=AZbKeLGu7UtY2xo6_Gj6p9qOMbmWxt__M3AzbAgA0jiO8xfm1aRfMgTtFAsr23BltUnrIArMgoIw_du2z-ayQ1trdJTvBJuLeEM1cvu8DihJ3-Anp9EsfUZthsefICIZfL1VU_F0yKNvo7XhfuO9zYRHnqOgXmB1JD594wLWwKqObw&__tn__=*b0H-R)

1

Tom Riedlinger

From a letter I sent to Tom Roberts in 2018: âI've just been browsing through files of letters I exchanged over 25 years ago with you and Albert Hofmann, and there appears to be no doubt that the first âformalâ Bicycle Day, which you hosted at your house in DeKalb (and which I attended), was on April 19th, 1985. It was news to Herr Hofmann that anyone anywhere was celebrating this event when you and I first wrote to him about it in the late 1980s. (In charmingly concrete fashion, he expressed what appears to be genuine surprise that his bicycle rather than LSD itself should be remembered on that day.) Also worth noting is an email message I received from our mutual friend Robert Forte on April 13, 1995, which states, in part: âHats off to Tom Roberts for coining âBicycle Day,â but credit must also go synchronicity [synchronistically?] for an unruly band of hippys in Santa Cruz [who] mass mailed an invitationâ in 1983 that called for âa planetary day of sacramentâ on April 16th with celebrants encouraged to âget out of your routine for a day, be in your own space, and in your own way, make this day special.â No mention of bicycles, though.â In any case, Paul, Happy Bicycle Day 2025âand thank you for sharing that photo of you and Albert with Jonathan Ott seated next to him.

Tom Riedlinger

From a letter I sent to Tom Roberts in 2018: âI've just been browsing through files of letters I exchanged over 25 years ago with you and Albert Hofmann, and there appears to be no doubt that the first âformalâ Bicycle Day, which you hosted at your house in DeKalb (and which I attended), was on April 19th, 1985. It was news to Herr Hofmann that anyone anywhere was celebrating this event when you and I first wrote to him about it in the late 1980s. (In charmingly concrete fashion, he expressed what appears to be genuine surprise that his bicycle rather than LSD itself should be remembered on that day.) Also worth noting is an email message I received from our mutual friend Robert Forte on April 13, 1995, which states, in part: âHats off to Tom Roberts for coining âBicycle Day,â but credit must also go synchronicity [synchronistically?] for an unruly band of hippys in Santa Cruz [who] mass mailed an invitationâ in 1983 that called for âa planetary day of sacramentâ on April 16th with celebrants encouraged to âget out of your routine for a day, be in your own space, and in your own way, make this day special.â No mention of bicycles, though.â In any case, Paul, Happy Bicycle Day 2025âand thank you for sharing that photo of you and Albert with Jonathan Ott seated next to him.
